zh.lifeSummary: 當Lucy Agnes BALLARD於1541年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼時，她的父親William BALLARD24歲，母親Agnes ROBERTSON20歲。她於大約1560年與Sir Richard WARD在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1589年6月23日在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其聖 彼得 曼可洛福特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lucy Agnes BALLARD于1541年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼时，她的父亲William BALLARD24岁，母亲Agnes ROBERTSON20岁。她于大约1560年与Sir Richard WARD在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1589年6月23日在英国諾薩普頓史爾享年48岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其聖 彼得 曼可洛福特。

nameMeanings.name: BALLARD
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ballard ‘bald-headed man’ (compare Bald 3).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Balhard, composed of the elements bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’, and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Ballor .

variants.name: Ballon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Sergio, Alfonso, Cesar, Conchita, Emigdio, Enrique, Felipe, Francisco, Jose Luis, Julio, Ruben.  Jewish Hyman, Isadore.

French and English (Middlesex): from the Old French personal name Ballon, Ballun, derived from the oblique stem of ancient Germanic Ballo, a short form of compound names based on the element bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’ (see Ballard ).

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Ballor
meanings: Altered form of French Ballard 2, reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. It is found mainly in MI.

variants.name: Bollard
meanings: English (Northamptonshire) and Irish: probably a variant of   Ballard  . According to MacLysaght, this is a surname of Dutch origin which was taken to Ireland early in the 18th century.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bolohard, composed of the elements bolo ‘friend, brother’ and hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Lucy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French Lucie, the vernacular form of Lucia . It is sometimes assumed that Lucy is a pet form of Lucinda , but there is no etymological justification for this assumption. It was in fairly widespread use in the Middle Ages, and increased greatly in popularity in the 18th century and again in the 1990s. In Ireland it serves as an Anglicized form of Irish Luíseach .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Lucy Agnes BALLARD was born in 1541, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, her father, William BALLARD, was 24 and her mother, Agnes ROBERTSON, was 20. She married Sir Richard WARD about 1560, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 23 June 1589, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 48, and was buried in St Peter Mancroft, Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Lucy Agnes BALLARDが1541年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイで生まれた時，父William BALLARDは24歳，母Agnes ROBERTSONは20歳であった。約1560年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイでSir Richard WARDと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1589年6月23日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチスト ペター マンクロフトにて埋葬された。

ko.St Peter Mancroft, Norwich, Norfolk, Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치 스트 피터 먼크로프트
ko.lifeSummary: Lucy Agnes BALLARD(이)가 1541년 영국 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William BALLARD(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes ROBERTSON(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1560년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 Sir Richard WARD(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1589년 6월 23일 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치 스트 피터 먼크로프트에 묻혔다.

ru.St Peter Mancroft, Norwich, Norfolk, Eng: ст питир манкрофт, норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lucy Agnes BALLARD родилась в 1541 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени William BALLARD было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes ROBERTSON, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard WARD около 1560 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 23 июня 1589 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности ст питир манкрофт, норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
